title: armeria canescens subsp. ruscinonensis
intro: Armeria canescens subsp. ruscinonensis, also known as Ruscin Purple thrift, is a subspecies of the perennial plant species Armeria canescens. It is a flowering plant that belongs to the Plumbaginaceae family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and ease respiratory issues. Armeria canescens subsp. ruscinonensis can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits and promote overall health and well-being.
